jobToBeDone: Help the user complete this narrow job: search api for developer workflows.
descriptionCandidate: Search API Developer for search api for developer workflows. Use when: the user needs this exact result quickly. Supports a fast first-run success path and obvious next actions.

viralUpgradePath.ordinaryVersion: Broad positioning, generic title, unclear first-run value.
viralUpgradePath.improvedVersion: Narrow use case, clear promise, more direct title.
viralUpgradePath.viralVersion: Exact intent title, instant first success, stronger proof of value, visible monetization hook.

contentCharacteristics: Lead with the job, not the implementation.
Make the first prompt succeed without heavy setup.
Keep input requirements low and output certainty high.
Use search-intent vocabulary in title and description.
Design one obvious upgrade reason from the first version.

structure.nameRule: Use exact user-intent terms, not internal architecture language.
structure.descriptionRule: State what it does, when to use it, and what it supports.
structure.exampleRule: The first example must succeed with one prompt.
structure.outputRule: Return concrete, decision-ready output rather than vague capability prose.

monetizationHooks: Free: small result set or capped usage.
Pro: real-time freshness or better quality output.
Pro: batch mode or automation depth.
Team: governance, logs, and shared credits.
launchChecklist: Track install velocity and repeat usage.
Design 5 adjacent variants from the same capability core.
operationalSteps: Day 1: validate the narrow user problem and choose one API/runtime.
Day 2: draft title, description, and first-run prompt flow.
Day 3: implement the minimal successful path only.
Day 4: package for ClawHub with examples and clear 'when to use'.
Day 5-7: release, observe usage, and prepare adjacent variants.
examplePrompts: Use Search API Developer to handle: search api for developer workflows
